project.title: Global profiling of lysine lactylation in prostate cancer cells
project.projectDescription: Lysine lactylation (Kla) is a recently identified  post-translational modification derived from lactate that regulates diverse biological processes. Although Kla has been studied in several cancers, its role  in prostate cancer (PC) remains unclear. We identified 681 Kla sites across 379 proteins, with modifications predominantly located in nuclear and cytoplasmic proteins. Enrichment analysis indicatedrevealed Kla involvement in mRNA splicing, chromatin organization, and glycolysis/gluconeogenesis.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: We performed global Kla profiling in PC-3M prostate cancer cells using affinity enrichment with anti-Kla antibodies, followed by LC-MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Bioinformatics analyses were conducted to explore the functional role of Kla -modified proteins.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: trypsin (20 µg; Promega Corporation, Madison, WI, USA)
Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic: Three replicate sets of the biological samples were prepared.
Comment[Instrument]: LTQ-Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[Separation]: nano-liquid chromatography system (Eksigent, Dublin, CA, USA)
Comment[IonizationType]: The electrospray ionization source was operated at a voltage of 1.8 kV.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: permitting up to two missed cleavage sites.
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: antibody-conjugated beads specific for Kla (PTM Biolab)
